title: Add more color schemes
close_reason: User decided to remove this task

title: Port this build over to solid-js from react
description:
## Scope
Port the renderer from React to Solid while preserving existing Electron behavior, workspace flows, and information architecture.

This epic covers:
- renderer boot and provider composition
- router and navigation preservation
- local UI primitives and higher-level component surfaces
- hook and context migration
- deletion of the in-app browser preview instead of porting it
- final verification, test migration, and React cleanup

## Design
- Keep `src/main/**`, preload IPC ownership, and Electron process boundaries intact unless a child task explicitly removes dead preview code.
- Preserve current product behavior and route structure rather than redesigning the UI during the framework migration.
- Prefer Solid equivalents for libraries the app already uses conceptually, especially TanStack router/query patterns, instead of swapping paradigms mid-migration.
- Break work into implementation-sized tasks that map to real renderer boundaries so later coding agents can execute them without re-scoping.

## Success Criteria
- The app boots and major workspace flows still work under Solid.
- The renderer no longer depends on React runtime packages.
- The in-app browser preview surface is removed and replaced with existing external-browser flows.
- Build, type-check, tests, and a smoke pass complete after the migration slices land.
notes: Blocked on emdash-onx.10. This cannot be represented as a blocks dependency because emdash-onx.10 is already a child of the epic, which would create a cycle.

title: Remove in-app browser preview
description:
## Scope
Remove the embedded browser preview surface instead of porting it to Solid.

Touch the browser-preview stack end to end:
- renderer files such as `src/renderer/components/BrowserPane.tsx`, `src/renderer/components/titlebar/BrowserToggleButton.tsx`, `src/renderer/components/BrowserPreviewSettingsCard.tsx`, `src/renderer/views/Workspace.tsx`, and `src/renderer/providers/BrowserProvider.tsx`
- preload/browser IPC in `src/main/preload.ts` and `src/main/ipc/browserIpc.ts`
- main-process service and window configuration in `src/main/services/browserViewService.ts` and `src/main/app/window.ts`
- preview settings and preview-specific telemetry in `src/main/settings.ts`, `src/main/telemetry.ts`, and related tests/types

## Design
- Replace any remaining preview/open actions with the existing `openExternal` path or host-preview-only behavior where appropriate.
- Delete preview-specific APIs rather than leaving deprecated wrappers behind.
- Remove `webviewTag` support from the main window once the renderer no longer embeds the preview surface.
- Update TypeScript declarations and tests alongside the removal so later migration tasks do not need preview compatibility shims.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No renderer path mounts `BrowserPane`, `BrowserProvider`, or the browser preview toggle/settings UI.
- Preload and main process no longer expose or handle `browser:view:*` APIs.
- The window configuration no longer enables the webview tag for the removed preview feature.
- Link-opening and localhost-preview flows still have an intentional behavior after the removal.

title: Primitives: forms, inputs, and small content controls
description:
## Scope
Port the form, input, status, and small content primitives under `src/renderer/components/ui` that are broadly reused across the app.

Start with files such as:
- `button.tsx`
- `input.tsx`
- `textarea.tsx`
- `checkbox.tsx`
- `radio-group.tsx`
- `select.tsx`
- `label.tsx`
- `badge.tsx`
- `card.tsx`
- `progress.tsx`
- `separator.tsx`
- `spinner.tsx`
- `shortcut-hint.tsx`
- `slug-input.tsx`
- `relative-time.tsx`
- `pr-status-skeleton.tsx`

## Design
- Preserve local exports and styling conventions so app surfaces can keep importing from `@/components/ui/*`.
- Replace React-only refs/events/composition patterns with Solid equivalents without widening the API.
- Keep this task focused on reusable leaf primitives rather than app-specific feature components.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted primitive files compile and run without React imports.
- Their public props and import paths remain stable enough for downstream component migration tasks.

title: Primitives: overlays, layout, and shared wrappers
description:
## Scope
Port the overlay, disclosure, navigation, and shell-oriented primitives under `src/renderer/components/ui`.

Start with files such as:
- `accordion.tsx`
- `alert-dialog.tsx`
- `alert.tsx`
- `breadcrumb.tsx`
- `collapsible.tsx`
- `context-menu.tsx`
- `dialog.tsx`
- `markdown-renderer.tsx`
- `popover.tsx`
- `scroll-area.tsx`
- `resizable.tsx`
- `sidebar.tsx`
- `right-sidebar.tsx`
- `toast.tsx`
- `toaster.tsx`
- `tooltip.tsx`

## Design
- Preserve the app-owned wrapper layer so higher-level component tasks are insulated from package churn.
- Keep accessibility and keyboard behavior on parity with the current wrappers.
- Treat `markdown-renderer` and shell/sidebar wrappers as part of the shared UI surface because many app features depend on them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted overlay/layout primitives no longer depend on React.
- App surfaces can continue importing them from the existing local module paths.
- Overlay/sidebar behavior remains compatible with current keyboard and layout flows.

title: Hooks: app-state and workspace management
description:
## Scope
Migrate the workspace/app-state hook layer to Solid while preserving the contracts consumed by providers and shell components.

Likely files include:
- `useLocalStorage.ts`
- `useTheme.ts`
- `useFeatureFlag.ts`
- `usePanelLayout.ts`
- `useWorkspaceConnection.ts`
- `useProjectManagement.tsx`
- `useTaskManagement.ts`
- `useTaskSettings.ts`
- `useTaskStatus.ts`
- `useTaskUnread.ts`
- `useStatusUnread.ts`
- `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts`
- `useDeleteRisks.ts`

Router-specific navigation hooks belong in `emdash-onx.7`, not here.

## Design
- Keep return shapes and state semantics stable so provider/component migration can happen incrementally.
- Replace React memo/effect/ref patterns with Solid primitives only where the hook contract requires them.
- Avoid mixing PR/file/terminal domains into this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted app-state hooks no longer depend on React hook APIs.
- Existing provider/component consumers can migrate without inventing new state contracts.

title: Hooks: file, git, review, and PR workflows
description:
## Scope
Migrate the file, git, review, and pull-request hook layer to Solid.

Likely files include:
- `useFileIndex.ts`
- `useFileManager.ts`
- `useFileDiff.ts`
- `useFileChanges.ts`
- `useTaskChanges.ts`
- `usePullRequests.ts`
- `useCreatePR.tsx`
- `useCheckRuns.ts`
- `useAutoCheckRunsRefresh.ts`
- `useAutoPrRefresh.ts`
- `usePrComments.ts`
- `usePrStatus.ts`
- `useLineComments.ts`
- `useDiffEditorComments.ts`
- `useEditorDiffDecorations.ts`
- `useOpenInApps.ts`

## Design
- Preserve async loading/error semantics expected by file/diff/review components.
- Keep network and electron-API integration logic inside the existing hook boundaries when those boundaries still make sense.
- Do not absorb terminal, automation, or project/workspace state hooks into this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted git/file/review hooks compile without React hooks.
- Their public return contracts remain compatible with the existing component layer.

title: Hooks: terminal, agent, automation, and remote workflows
description:
## Scope
Migrate the terminal, agent, automation, and remote/integration hook layer to Solid.

Likely files include:
- `useAgentEvents.ts`
- `useAgentPreference.ts`
- `useTaskAgentNames.ts`
- `useAutomationTrigger.ts`
- `useChangelogNotification.ts`
- `useUpdateNotifier.tsx`
- `useUpdater.ts`
- `useRemoteProject.ts`
- `useSshConnections.ts`
- `useTerminalSearch.ts`
- `useTerminalSelection.ts`
- `useTerminalViewportWheelForwarding.ts`
- `useContentSearch.ts`
- `useInitialPromptInjection.ts`
- `useCommentInjection.ts`
- `useCliAgentDetection.ts`
- `useTelemetryConsent.ts`
- `useConversationStatus.ts`
- `useAutoScrollOnTaskSwitch.ts`
- `useIsMonochrome.ts`

## Design
- Preserve event-subscription and cleanup behavior carefully; many of these hooks bridge renderer state with terminals, IPC, or remote connections.
- Keep this slice focused on non-router, non-PR, non-core-app-state hooks.
- Favor stable contracts for downstream terminal/chat/integration components.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The targeted terminal/agent/integration hooks no longer rely on React hook APIs.
- Subscription cleanup and event lifecycles remain explicit and Solid-compatible.

title: Components: shell, navigation, and settings surfaces
description:
## Scope
Port the shell, navigation, and settings surfaces that define the overall application frame.

Own components such as:
- `src/renderer/views/Workspace.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/titlebar/**`
- `src/renderer/components/sidebar/**`
- `src/renderer/components/MainContent.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/MainContentArea.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/HomeView.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/SettingsPage.tsx`
- settings cards and shared shell helpers such as `CommandPalette*`, `ExternalLinkModal`, `ModalRenderer`, and `ThemeProvider`

Browser preview UI owned by `emdash-onx.10` is removed, not ported here.

## Design
- Preserve the current workspace frame, titlebar/sidebar behavior, and settings-navigation flow.
- Use migrated primitive/provider/router layers rather than introducing local compatibility wrappers in these shell components.
- Keep this task focused on the application frame, not task-detail or editor/diff surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The shell/navigation/settings surface compiles without React.
- Workspace frame behavior remains compatible with current sidebar, titlebar, command palette, and settings flows.
- Browser preview UI is absent rather than silently reintroduced.

title: Components: task and project management surfaces
description:
## Scope
Port the task/project/workspace management surfaces that drive the core product workflows.

Own components such as:
- `ProjectMainView.tsx`
- `TaskList.tsx`, `TaskItem.tsx`, `TaskModal.tsx`
- `TaskSettings*`, `TaskChanges.tsx`, `TaskHoverActionCard.tsx`
- `BranchSelect.tsx`, `BaseBranchControls.tsx`
- `OpenPrsSection.tsx`, `MergePrSection.tsx`, `FileChangesPanel.tsx`
- project/task CRUD and move dialogs
- project/workspace cards tightly coupled to task/project management

## Design
- Preserve current task/project workflow behavior, selection state, and bulk-action semantics.
- Build on the migrated primitives, provider layer, and git/file/review hooks instead of re-owning that logic in components.
- Exclude chat/terminal/editor/diff specialty surfaces from this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The core task/project management surface compiles without React.
- Task/project flows still operate through the existing hooks/providers and maintain the current UX semantics.

title: Components: chat, terminal, editor, and integration surfaces
description:
## Scope
Port the specialized feature surfaces that sit on top of the shared shell and state layers.

Own components/directories such as:
- `ChatInterface.tsx`, `ChatTabs.tsx`, related review/chat helpers
- terminal surfaces including `TerminalPane.tsx`, `TaskTerminalPanel.tsx`, `LifecycleTerminalView.tsx`
- `diff-viewer/**`
- `FileExplorer/**`
- `monaco/**`
- `kanban/**`
- `automations/**`
- `ssh/**`
- `mcp/**` and `skills/**`
- issue selectors/tooltips, integration setup forms/cards, and other specialized feature components not covered by `emdash-onx.16` or `emdash-onx.17`

`BrowserPane.tsx` is deleted in `emdash-onx.10` instead of being ported here.

## Design
- Keep this task bounded to specialized renderer surfaces whose complexity comes from terminals, editors, integrations, or rich feature workflows.
- Depend on the migrated primitive, hook, provider, router, and browser-preview-removal work instead of carrying fallback React shims.
- Preserve existing feature behavior before optimizing or redesigning any surface.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The specialized renderer surfaces compile without React.
- Browser preview components are removed rather than migrated.
- Terminal/editor/integration surfaces continue to use the existing domain hooks and IPC boundaries after the framework swap.

title: Build & dependency swap
description:
## Scope
Replace the React-specific renderer toolchain and package set with Solid equivalents without changing the Electron main-process build.

Touch:
- `package.json` renderer dependencies and devDependencies
- `vite.config.ts`
- `tsconfig.json`
- renderer test harness dependencies and JSX/runtime configuration

## Design
- Swap the renderer to a Solid JSX runtime and Solid Vite plugin.
- Preserve the existing alias structure, renderer root, and main-process TypeScript builds.
- Migrate TanStack integrations to the Solid packages instead of replacing router/query architecture during the same task.
- Keep this task focused on package/config readiness; do not start porting feature code here.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A clean install succeeds with the Solid renderer dependency set.
- Renderer `.tsx` files compile through the Solid JSX runtime in dev, build, and test config.
- React runtime packages are removed from the intended end state or clearly isolated as temporary follow-up items.
- Downstream migration tasks can import Solid packages without additional config work.

title: Entry point & foundation
description:
## Scope
Port the renderer boot path and shared foundation so later feature slices have a stable Solid entry point.

Touch the renderer shell files that currently define application startup and provider composition:
- `src/renderer/main.tsx`
- `src/renderer/App.tsx`
- `src/renderer/AppShell.tsx`
- any JSX-runtime-sensitive icon/bootstrap helpers touched by those files

## Design
- Replace `ReactDOM.createRoot(...).render(...)` with the Solid render entry point.
- Rebuild the top-level app shell so provider ordering and first-launch/workspace routing behavior remain intact.
- Keep the boundary between framework-agnostic utilities and renderer-framework code explicit so child tasks know where Solid should stop.
- Do not absorb router migration, provider internals, or feature-surface porting into this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The renderer boots through a Solid entry point.
- The top-level shell still routes first-launch users to the welcome flow and everyone else to the workspace flow.
- Child tasks have a stable Solid app shell to build against without redefining startup behavior.

title: UI primitives migration
description:
## Scope
Replace the local React-based UI primitives in `src/renderer/components/ui` with Solid-compatible implementations while keeping the app-owned import surface stable for downstream component tasks.

This epic is split so later agents can port primitives in coherent families instead of editing the full `components/ui` tree in one pass.

## Design
- Preserve local component names and import paths whenever possible.
- Port primitive families first, then let higher-level app surfaces migrate against those local wrappers.
- Keep styling conventions (`cn`, class-variance-authority patterns, Tailwind classes) aligned with the current repo instead of redesigning primitives.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks cover the full primitive surface under `src/renderer/components/ui`.
- App-level component tasks can migrate against local Solid primitives rather than importing framework packages directly.

title: Hook migration
description:
## Scope
Replace React hook implementations in `src/renderer/hooks` with Solid primitives while preserving the consumer-facing contracts that the component layer already uses.

This epic is split by domain so later agents can migrate hooks without reopening scope for unrelated feature areas.

## Design
- Preserve hook/module boundaries where they are still useful to the app.
- Convert state/effect/subscription logic to Solid primitives without redesigning product behavior.
- Keep router-specific hook work with the router migration task instead of scattering navigation changes across unrelated hook tasks.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks cover the current renderer hook surface in domain-sized slices.
- Downstream component tasks can adopt Solid-backed hooks without inventing new state APIs.

title: Context providers migration
description:
## Scope
Port the renderer context/provider layer to Solid while preserving the existing provider ordering and public context hooks.

Primary files:
- `src/renderer/contexts/AppContextProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/AppSettingsProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/EmdashAccountProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/ExternalLinkContext.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/GitWorkspaceBusyContext.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/GithubContextProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/KeyboardSettingsContext.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/ModalProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/ProjectManagementProvider.tsx`
- `src/renderer/contexts/TaskManagementContext.tsx`

`BrowserProvider` is intentionally removed in `emdash-onx.10` instead of being ported here.

## Design
- Preserve the imperative APIs exposed by providers such as modal management and settings updates.
- Keep provider composition compatible with `AppShell` after the Solid entry-point task lands.
- Port context consumers to Solid-compatible hooks/signals inside the provider modules; do not absorb unrelated component work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The listed provider/context files no longer depend on React context or React hooks.
- Provider order and externally consumed context APIs remain compatible with the rest of the renderer.
- Browser preview provider code is not reintroduced as part of this task.

title: Router and navigation migration
description:
## Scope
Migrate routing and route-aware navigation helpers to Solid while preserving the current hash-based workspace URLs and overlay flows.

Primary files:
- `src/renderer/App.tsx`
- `src/renderer/router.tsx`
- `src/renderer/hooks/useWorkspaceRouter.ts`
- any tightly-coupled route helper usage needed to keep `workspaceRoutes` behavior intact

## Design
- Preserve the existing route inventory and hash-history navigation model.
- Keep the TanStack router approach already present in the app, but switch to the Solid package surface.
- Ensure route-driven overlays such as settings and diff views still open/close via URL state rather than local modal-only state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The app exposes the same route shapes and route params after the migration.
- Workspace navigation helpers still generate and consume the existing href/search patterns.
- Route-driven overlay flows continue to round-trip through the URL correctly.

title: Application surfaces migration
description:
## Scope
Port renderer components outside the primitive layer in vertical slices after foundations land.

This epic deliberately excludes `components/ui/**` and excludes the in-app browser preview, which is removed in `emdash-onx.10`.

## Design
- Migrate app surfaces in slices that match real feature ownership instead of attempting a single pass across ~200 component files.
- Keep current product behavior, layout, and module boundaries unless a child task has a repo-grounded reason to adjust them.
- Let each child task name the feature surfaces it owns so later agents can work without re-triaging the whole component tree.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks cover the app-specific component surface in implementation-sized slices.
- The remaining React-dependent component work is discoverable by child issue rather than hidden in one catch-all task.

title: Validation, test migration, and React removal
description:
## Scope
Finish the migration by updating the test harness, removing remaining React-only leftovers, and running full verification.

Touch:
- renderer/component test utilities and imports under `src/test/**`
- any renderer config or helper files still carrying React-specific compatibility code
- final search-and-destroy for stale React imports after the migration slices land

## Design
- Replace React-only component test setup with Solid-compatible testing utilities.
- Run broad repo checks only after the leaf migration tasks are complete so failures point to real regressions instead of half-migrated code.
- Treat this task as cleanup/verification, not a dumping ground for unfinished feature migration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `pnpm type-check`, `pnpm test`, and `pnpm build` pass for the migrated codebase.
- Remaining React imports/runtime deps are removed from renderer code and tests, or any intentional exception is documented explicitly.
- A smoke pass covers the primary workspace flow after the Solid migration lands.

title: Fix authentication bug
description: Login fails with special characters in password
